1.8 We reiterated our recommendations and Management agreed to:
a. Prioritize the full and immediate implementation of the guidelines set
forth in COA Circular No. 2020-006, particularly the one-time
cleansing of PPE accounts. This is necessary to establish verifiable
balances that reflect the true existence, condition, and accountability of
PPE items.
b. Issue a directive requiring the Inventory Committee to complete the
physical count of all PPE within three months from the receipt of this
memorandum.
c. Instruct the Inventory Committee to submit the approved Physical
Inventory Plan to the Audit Team at least ten (10) days before inventory
activities begin, ensuring it is fully executed.
d. Direct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to reconcile PPE
listings with office records, update the ledger and property cards, and
tag all items after inventory.

2.11 We recommended and the Municipal Engineer agreed to provide the
Municipal Accountant and the GSO-Designee with a complete description
and cost segregation of the road components for road projects.
2.12 We also recommended and the Municipal Treasurer, as GSO-Designee,
agreed to maintain Local Road Network Property Cards for all roads and
their components, and a Local Road Inventory and Road Map. We also
recommended and Management agreed that an inventory be conducted each
year and the corresponding Report on the Physical Count of the Road
Network System be prepared and submitted to the Auditor as prescribed
under COA Circular No. 2015-008.
2.13 We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to
maintain Local Road Network Ledger Cards with complete descriptions and
reconcile the same with the Local Road Inventory and Road Map to ensure
completeness, as well as to recognize depreciation of the depreciable
components thereof and provide disclosures on the account in the Notes to
Financial Statements.

3.16 We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal
Accountant to conduct a comprehensive review and analysis of the recorded
A/Ps as of year-end, and recommend reverting to Unappropriated Surplus
in the General Fund all those that have remained outstanding for two years
or more, for which no actual claims, whether administrative or judicial, have
been filed, or which are not covered by perfected contracts on record in
accordance with Section 98 of P.D. No. 1445 and DBM-COA Joint Circular
No. 99-06 dated November 13, 1999, so that these may be made available to
finance other developmental projects and activities.
3.17 Further, we recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Ensure that obligations incurred are properly recorded in the books of
the Municipality as soon as the goods are accepted or services rendered
and the corresponding bills are received from the contractor or supplier;
b. Require that obligations shall be covered with duly approved DVs
supported with the required documents, such as the Inspection and
Acceptance Report and bills as received, as the basis for recording of the
payables in the books; and
c. Require the maintenance and monitoring of subsidiary ledgers with
complete details for tracking and verification.

4.12 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer agreed to maintain supplies ledger cards and stock cards,
respectively, in accordance with Section 114 of the Manual on the NGAS for
LGUs Volume I.
4.13 We also recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor
issue an Executive Order for the creation of the Inventory Committee, which
will conduct the actual count of inventory items and prepare a Report on the
Physical Count of Inventories semi-annually in compliance with Section 124
of the same Manual.
4.14 Lastly, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant, the Municipal
Treasurer, the Municipal Agricultural Officer, and the Municipal Health
Officer agreed to conduct regular reconciliation of the actual inventory
count against the balances in the books of account to ascertain the booked
balances of the inventory items.

5.12 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
a.) Send demand letters to officers and employees, copy furnish the Audit
Team, to immediately settle their cash advances in accordance with the
pertinent sections of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997;
b.) Ensure that all cash advances are fully liquidated at the end of each year,
if warranted;
c.) Stop the practice of granting multiple cash advances to a particular
employee with previous cash advances that are not yet properly
liquidated;
d.) Require AOs to properly liquidate and promptly settle/ liquidate their
cash advances and, in case they fail, consider imposing sanctions such as
withholding of their salaries as provided in the Circular;
e.) Monitor and strictly enforce the liquidation of cash advances within the
prescribed period, particularly to AOs and employees who are still active
in service; and
f.) Conduct re-evaluation/validation of all outstanding accounts and, if
warranted, request for the write-off of long-overdue cash advances that
can no longer be collected in accordance with the procedures provided
under COA Circular No. 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023.

We recommended that Management undertake the following measures so as to fairly present the Property, Plant and Equipment Account in conformity with the above-mentioned provisions:

Require the Municipal Accounting Office to prepare and periodically update the schedule of items composing the PPE accounts, together with pertinent data such as the acquisition cost, acquisition date and estimated useful life. 

Direct both the Municipal Accountant to prepare Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Card (PPELC) in consonance with the MNGAS for LGU.

Undertake regular reconciliation of accounting and GSO records and promptly adjust any discrepancies noted to ensure complete and reliable PPE account balances at any given reporting period.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Constantly review and monitor the PPE Lapsing Schedule and evaluate the correctness of the data using the underlying provisions and basis of computation in IPSAS 17, and other generally accepted accounting principles; 

Prepare a lapsing schedule and provide depreciation to specified PPE accounts without schedule and corresponding accumulated depreciation; and

Prepare the appropriate journal entries, duly supported by working paper computations, to correct the depreciation and effect the necessary adjustments in order to rectify the errors and present the asset accounts at their proper carrying amounts.

We recommended to Management the following remedial measures in compliance with IPSAS No. 17 and COA Circular No. 92-386:
To designate a focal person to exhaust all possible means to facilitate the processing on the legality of ownership of the lots through a Transfer Certificate of Title (TCT) in the name of the Municipal Government of Villanueva with due consideration of the memorandum of encumbrance, and seek budgetary allocations for the pertinent cost thereof; and
To secure the notarized Deed of Donation and to provide copies of TCT and duly approved Deed of Donation to the Municipal Accountant, as basis for the latter to establish a subsidiary list; and
After thorough verification on the duly approved deed of donation, direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting entry for the derecognition of the donated portion of the Land Account.

We recommend that Management:
Compel all the accountable officers or personnel with unliquidated cash advances to submit immediately the necessary documents and cashbooks/ records to fully account and liquidate their long overdue accounts and instruct the Accountant to monitor closely the liquidation of these cash advances in accordance with the reglementary periods prescribed under Section 1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001;
Require all concerned Accountable Officers to return immediately all unspent cash advances, and in case of non-compliance, instruct the Accountant and the Treasurer to deduct and withhold from the salaries and allowances of the concerned SDOs, an amount to satisfy or settle their unliquidated advances; and
If warranted, initiate the filing of appropriate administrative charge against the concerned SDOs for their failure to render their accounts under Sections 89 and 128 of P.D. 1445 and Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code.

subsidiary ledgers, as mandated by Section 114(2) of P.D. No. 1445; and
c) Comply with Section 10 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, which necessitates
submitting the required subsidiary ledgers to substantiate the account
balances reported in the financial statements.

4.13 We recommended and Management agreed that:
a) The Municipal Assessor furnish the MTO with the Assessment Roll as the basis
for the collection of real property taxes and for updating the RPTAR/ Index
Card;
b) The Municipal Treasurer’s Office assign a personnel in-charge of the regular
updating of the RPTAR/TIC and maintain a list showing the names of the
taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year to be furnished to the
Municipal Accounting Office;
c) The Municipal Accountant set up tax receivables and deferred tax income
based on the certified list of taxpayers with the amount due and collectible in
the current year submitted by the Municipal Treasurer;
d) The Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary ledgers for accounts RPT
Receivable and SET Receivable in accordance with Section 10 of the NGAS
Manual for LGUs, Volume II; and
e) The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer reconcile the difference
between the RPT and SET Receivables account balances totaling
₱1,576,798.71 and henceforth, conduct periodic reconciliation, together with the
Municipal Assessor, of the total collectibles from the Basic and Special
Education Tax with the balances of the RPT Receivables and SET Receivables
accounts to avoid the accumulation of unreconciled differences.

5.8 We recommended and Management agreed that the:
a. Bids and Awards Committee ensure that the Municipality’s Annual
Procurement Plan schedules the procurement, delivery, and completion of
goods and services within the same year;
b. Municipal Accountant stop the practice of recording amounts due to
suppliers or contractors for goods or services not yet received and ensure
that obligations incurred are only recorded in the books of the Municipality as
soon as the goods are accepted or services rendered and the corresponding
bills are received from the contractor or supplier; and
c. Municipal Accountant ensure that obligations are covered with duly
approved DVs supported with the required documents such as the
Inspection and Acceptance Report and bills as received as the basis for
recording the payables in the books in compliance with Section 4(s) of the
NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I, to fairly present the A/P account in the
financial statements at year-end.

6.8 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the
necessary adjusting journal entries to rectify the misstatements in the affected
accounts by:
a) derecognizing the amount of the parts replaced; and
b) recognizing the cost of the repairs and maintenance through the proposed
entry below in compliance with IPSAS 17:
Dr. Other Structures -----------------------------------------------₱490,182.17
Cr. Accumulated Surplus -----------------------------------------₱490,182.17

6.9 We further recommended that when preparing the Annual Budget, the
Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Planning and Development Officer
agreed to carefully determine whether the identified project, program, or
activity falls under repairs and maintenance as part of maintenance and other
operating expenses or if they should be considered as capital expenditures.

7.9 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to evaluate and
identify dormant receivable accounts in which collectability proved to be futile
after exhausting all possible means to collect and file a request for authority to
write off to the Commission on Audit pursuant to COA Circular No. 2023-008.

8.13 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
a. Maintain the SLs for the Due to NGAs account duly indicating the
pertinent information such as the date when the fund was received, the
source agency, the purpose, and the amount received, utilized, and
liquidated;
b. Ensure that fund transfers recorded under the Trust Fund are duly
supported by a MOA; and
c. Return the excess funds for the fully implemented/completed projects to
the respective SAs accounts under the Bureau of Treasury.

9.13 We recommended and Management agreed to discontinue the employment of a
private lawyer or legal consultant and strictly comply with the provisions of COA
Circular No. 92-008 and Section 481 of R.A. No. 7160.

10.13 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
a. Prepare the necessary accounting entries to transfer from the GF to the
Special Trust Fund all the unexpended/unobligated QRF and the DRRMF-
MOOE balances amounting to ₱1,776,656.60; and
b. Review the previous years’ balances to identify the appropriate balances
for the unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE and prepare the
appropriate adjusting entries to transfer from the GF to the trust fund in
accordance with Section 5.1.10 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

11.7 We recommended Management agreed that the LDRRM Council pass a
resolution reverting the unutilized funds that have already lapsed the five-year
validity period to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund to be made
available for other social services in conformity with Section 5.1.13 of COA
Circular No. 2012-002.

We also recommended that Management submit a justification/explanation for those expenses which are contrary to the above mentioned Joint Circular.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the following necessary documents for evaluation to cause the settlement of the remaining unliquidated cash advances of the Municipality for CY 2015,to wit:

2 demand letters to officials and employees with remaining cash advances.
Certification from the Municipal Mayor or any other official that can certify that the said official or employee cannot be found nor traced.
And Death Certificate for deceased officials or employees.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary ledgers for each official and employee granted with cash advances to facilitate easy monitoring of balances to prevent the release of another cash advance without the settlement or proper accounting of the previous cash advance. The subsidiary ledgers should also be used as basis for the approval of clearances of officials or employees with cash advances.

Likewise, the standard form of the Report on Ageing of CA should be used by the Municipality illustrated in Annex 3 of Memorandum Circular No. 2012-02-A dated October 31, 2012, an Administrative Order No.25 S. 2011.

We recommended that Management create an inventory committee to conduct the physical inventory of property and to submit the required inventory report to the Local Chief Executive copy furnish the Office of the Auditor. Thereafter, instruct the Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer to maintain property records and to reconcile this with the inventory records.

We also recommended the submission of the lapsing schedule every year.
observations.reason: The Inventory Report was already submitted to the COA Office but the balances were not yet reconciled with the accounting records

observations.recommendation:
We recommended Management to observe strictly Section 272 of RA 7160 and the implementation of DECS-DBM-DILG JC no.1, as amended on the SEF utilization.

We also recommended the Management to submit proof that expenses incurred above the appropriation is properly authorized and give justification/explanation for payments made for Representation Expenses-District Supervisor’s Office, Electricity, Repairs & Maintenance of Motor Vehicle, Drugs & Medicines, Communication (other than telephone landline),Climate Change Mitigation: Supplies/Medicines/Drugs.

Expenses not in accordance with the above stated provisions may be suspended/disallowed in audit.

We recommended that approved contracts together with its complete supporting documents of the abovementioned procurement of infrastructure be submitted to the Office of the Auditor immediately to enable us to review and make decisions in audit, otherwise transactions covered by the unsubmitted documents will be suspended in audit.

We also required that approved contracts together with its supporting documents for this year 2016 be submitted within 5 days from the execution thereof.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the GAD Focal Point Person re-evaluate the items identified as GAD related PAPs in the GAD Budget prepared by the Municipal Budget Office so that items not related to GAD will be excluded from the GAD budget for the succeeding years.

The GAD Focal Point person should secure a copy of the Specific Guidelines Item B No. 2 of COA Circular 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014 that provides a guide on the Programs, Activities and Projects (PAPs) where the funds may be applied.

We also recommended that the management should submit a GAD Plan and Budget for the year 2016 that conforms to the rules and regulations on GAD Funds to promote economy, efficiency and effectiveness of GAD-related expenditures. The abovementioned templates should be followed to facilitate the proper charges to the GAD fund.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:
 
Limit the hiring of consultants on projects of such magnitude and/or scope which require a level of expertise or attention beyond the optimum in-house capacity of the agency so as to ensure the necessity of hiring them with specific output/ deliverable, and the compliance of the requirements of above-mentioned Section 9.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001, and related provisions of the IRR of RA 9184, its Annex “B” and the 2010 PBDs on consulting services.

Discontinue the services of those whose functions are redundant/ duplicating the services expected from the regular personnel, or their services have no distinct deliverables/outputs in accordance with delivery schedule which are among the requirements of Annex “B” of the revised IRR of RA 9184, and the 2010 PBDs on consulting services.

Require the submission of the authority of the Solicitor General and the concurrence of the COA Central Office on the hiring of legal consultants.

We recommended that the Budget Office maintain the registries and submit the Statement of Appropriations, Allotments, Obligations and Balances monthly to the Accounting Office before the 20th day of the next month for inclusion in the submission of financial reports by the Accounting Office to the Auditor.
 
We recommended that the Budget Office coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer’s Office for the availability of funds before the issuance of Allotment Advise to various offices.

We recommend that management:
 
Implement the projects and programs identified in the Annual Investment Plan under the 20% Development Fund. 

Require the designated department head to submit periodic reports on the status of programs/project implementation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accounting Office to:
Record back to the books of accounts of the Municipality the Public Infrastructures that were transferred to the registries under the NGAS amounting to ₱17,489,983 pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-004; and
Keep the personnel updated of the latest guidelines, accounting rules and regulations promulgated by COA.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accounting Office to start preparing the subsidiary ledgers (SLs) for CY 2018 using the transactions that may arise in the said year then moving backwards, year after year, after all the transactions pertaining to every applicable account in the GL are reflected in the SLs.
This may be facilitated by creating a special task force to augment the said office for the reconciliation of financial records in view of the minimal manpower complement of that office.
We also recommended that Management consider the procurement of computerized accounting systems to aid the Municipal Accounting Office in the maintenance of records which will redound to better financial management decision due to faster generation of reports and records.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:
Submit the vouchers, reports and supporting documents for the months of October to December 2017 for GF, SEF and TF;
Coordinate with the employees concerned in the preparation of supporting documents and install necessary measures for the complete submission and prompt consolidation of vouchers. Where applicable, attach a list of the identified deficiencies in the DV and attachments for compliance by the concerned departments; and
Strictly observe the reglementary period provided under COA Circular No. 2009-006.
We further recommended that Management cause the suspension of the salaries of concerned officials and employees until they shall have submitted the required documents to COA pursuant to Section 122 of PD 1445.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:
Strictly observe the reglementary period provided under PD 1445, COA Circular No. 2009-006 and NGAS Manual in the submission of monthly trial balances and interim reports; and
Coordinate with the employees concerned in the preparation and reconciliation of documents and ledgers for the timely consolidation and finalization of reports;
We further recommended that Management cause the suspension of the salaries of concerned officials and employees until they shall have submitted the required documents to COA pursuant to Section 122 of PD 1445.

We recommended that Management:
Transmit all contracts still pending for submission together with all supporting documents to facilitate contracts review;
Require the strict observance of the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001; and
Develop a systematic process of monitoring all the infrastructure projects of the Municipality to ensure timely submission of the contracts and each of all the documents forming part thereof. The format provided to the Municipal Engineering Office may be revised/enhanced to include the date when the contracts were submitted to COA, among other information that Management deems necessary for better decision making.

We recommended that Management:
Transmit all contracts still pending for submission together with all supporting documents to facilitate contract review;
Strictly observe the reglementary period provided under COA Circular No. 2009-001 in the submission of contracts for review, copies of purchase orders and all the required pertinent documents; and
Develop and maintain a procurement monitoring system that contains basic information about the projects to be undertaken and procurement timelines to ensure timely submission of contracts, purchase orders, and each of all the documents forming part thereof to COA and other regulatory bodies.

We recommended that Management:
Properly designate personnel in the MDRRMO as to who is/are responsible for: (i) administration and training; (ii) research and planning; and (iii) operations and warning, to assist the DRRMO in performing the functions of the LDRRMO in the Municipality in compliance with RA 10121;
Revise and prepare the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan(LDRRMFIP) in accordance with the devised Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Plan, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002; and
Ensure the timely submission of the Report on Sources and Utilization of the DRRMF to COA on or before the 15th day of the following month.

We recommended that Management:
Strictly observe and adhere to the policies and guidelines on the use and purpose of the Special Education Fund as provided under RA 7160 and DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1 s. 2017; and
Submit justification/explanation of the continued non-compliance of the above-stated guidelines.

We recommended that Management:
Submit immediately the lacking documents required under COA Circulars No. 2009-001 and 2012-001 dated February 12, 2009 and June 14, 2012, respectively, and submit an explanation for the deficiencies and inconsistencies noted in the contract review; and
Submit explanation from the Contractor for the incurrence of negative slippage of 17.71% and the Procuring Entity’s evaluation thereon and subsequent action.

We recommended that Management:
Submit written explanation for the non-implementation of projects charged against the 20% Development Fund which affected the full utilization of the fund to achieve a more desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the LGU;
Develop a more effective budget and investment plan that can adequately support the actual cost of project implementation and create functional measures and systems to closely monitor the status of project implementation; and
Properly identify all development projects in the Annual Investment Planand use consistent program/project/activity name for proper monitoring and to avoid confusion.

We recommended that:

1.	The Revenue Collecting Officers remit immediately the remaining collections still on hand and stop immediately the practice of not remitting their collections intact and to record all collections whether remitted or unremitted in the cashbook to reflect its correct balance.

2. 	Management effect the immediate relief of the defaulting Revenue Collecting Officer from his duties as Accountable Officer

3. 	Management direct the Municipal Treasurer/Liquidating Officer to verify the Report of Collections and Deposits; check the statement of accountable forms as to initial balances on hand, receipts, issuances and the ending balances on hand; make a physical count of the accountable forms remaining in the custody of the collector/teller and check the same against the new balances on hand column. No issuance of Accountable Forms should be made to Revenue Collecting Officer who has balances on hand, receipts and issues to monitor the collection and remittance of each and every RCD that the Collectors and Liquidating Officer submitted to her and use a different stub of AF 51 for the collection of each and every Fund. The Municipal Treasurer should keep a record which will enable her to identify which RCD is included for each and every deposit slip to give assurance that all collection are deposited intact. And she should also reflect the reference no./date of the RCD on the reference portion of the cashbook for easy verification.

4. 	Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to set up a system of internal control within the Municipal Treasurer’s Office that will ensure the remittance of the collection of every revenue collecting officers daily so that deposit of collections shall be made at least once a week, or as soon as the collections reach ₱10,000.00. As required in the abovementioned COA Circular. Since these are regular income of the municipality, this forms part of the cash back up of the appropriation in the annual budget. Implementation of Projects, programs and activities may be delayed due to limited availability of cash resulting from this deficiency, thus it is important that all collections be deposited intact immediately without delay.

5. 	As part of the internal control of the Municipality, all collectors, if possible, remit their collections to the Liquidating Officer to monitor full remittance.

We also recommended that Management require the immediate submission of the written explanation of the revenue Collectors why shortage occurred.
observations.reason: Remittances of collections are not done daily due to the use of cash collections for cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer properly bond all personnel of the Municipality whose duties and responsibilities permit or require the possession of custody of local funds, so that all collectors will maintain and record all their collection in their respective cashbooks. Moreover, we recommend that the Municipal Treasurer direct all sub-collectors to remit all their collections immediately to their designated bonded Revenue Collector and stop the practice of designating sub-collectors and set-up a strong internal control within the MTO.
observations.reason: N/A

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct and/or remind the Municipal Treasurer to strictly follow the rules and procedures set forth under the Local Treasury Operations Manual in discharging her functions to safeguard and provide adequate protection of the cash that goes to her custody.

We required the Municipal Treasurer to retain and maintain files of the Report of Collection and Deposit (RCD) of her collection, RCD of those collectors who remitted their collection to her, duplicate copies of Deposit Slips and any other documents that would readily show proof of her deposit for each and every RCD that the Collectors and Liquidating Officer submitted to her and use a different stub of AF 51 for the collection of each and every Fund. The Municipal Treasurer should keep a record which will enable her to identify which RCD is included for each and every deposit slip to give assurance that all collection are deposited intact. And she should also reflect the reference no./date of the RCD on the reference portion of the cashbook for easy verification.

We required the Municipal Treasurer to:
	
	a. Stop the issuance of receipts to “sub-collectors” for they are not bonded.

	b. Maintain an adequate permanent record book which also contains the column “date reported totally used, sold, issued” to readily determine the whereabouts of the Accountable Forms.

	c. Require her collectors to prepare and file the Requisition and Issue Slip and avoid the borrowing or transfer of Accountable Forms between collectors or “sub collectors”.

	d. Submit to the Office of the Auditor the Consolidated Report of Accountability of Accountable forms every 5th day of the ensuing month as required.

	e. Report immediately loss of accountable forms and submit letter request for its relief as required under Sec 100, Book II, Vol I, GAAM.

	f. Maintain separate cashbook for the DBP and LBP depository bank accounts under the General Fund of the Municipality and to reflect its beginning and ending balances every month.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly follow Section 43 and Section 46 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs to facilitate verification of entries in the JEV, Check Disbursement Journal and Cash Disbursement Journal.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
The Revenue Collecting Officers remit immediately the remaining collections still on hand and stop immediately the practice of not remitting their collections intact and to record all collections whether remitted or unremitted in the cashbook to reflect its correct balance.
Management effect the immediate relief of the defaulting Revenue Collecting Officer from his duties as Accountable Officer.
Management direct the Municipal Treasurer/Liquidating Officer to verify the Report of Collections and Deposits; check the statement of accountable forms as to initial balances on hand, receipts, issues and the ending balances on hand; make a physical count of the accountable forms remaining in the custody of the collector/teller and check the same against the new balances on hand column. No issuance of Accountable Forms should be made to Revenue Collecting Officer who has balances on hand, receipts and issues to monitor the collection and remittance of each Revenue Collector.
Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to set up a system of internal control within the Municipal Treasurer’s Office that will ensure the remittance of the collection of every revenue collecting officers daily so that deposit of collections shall be made at least once a week, or as soon as the collections reach P10,000.00. As required in the abovementioned COA Circular. Since these are regular income of the municipality, this forms part of the cash back up of the appropriation in the annual budget. Implementation of Projects, programs and activities may be delayed due to limited availability of cash resulting from this deficiency, thus it is important that all collections be deposited intact immediately without delay.
As part of the internal control of the Municipality, all collectors, if possible, remit their collections to the Liquidating Officer to monitor full remittance.
We also recommended that Management require the immediate submission of the written explanation of the Revenue Collectors why shortages occurred.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer properly bond all personnel of the Municipality whose duties and responsibilities permit or require the possession or custody of local funds, so that all collectors  will maintain and record all their collection in their respective cashbooks. Moreover, we recommend that the Municipal Treasurer direct all sub-collectors to remit all their collections immediately to their designated bonded Revenue Collector and stop the practice of designating sub-collectors and set up a strong internal control within the MTO.

We recommended that Management direct and/or remind the Municipal Treasurer to strictly follow the rules and procedures set forth under the Local Treasury Operations Manual in discharging her functions to safeguard and provide adequate protection of the cash that goes to her custody.
We required the Municipal Treasurer to retain and maintain files of the Report of Collection and Deposit (RCD) of her collection, RCD of those collectors who remitted their collection to her, duplicate copies of Deposit Slips and any other documents that would readily show proof of her deposit for each and every RCD that the Collectors and Liquidating Officer submitted to her and use a different stub of AF 51 for the collection of each and every Fund. The Municipal Treasurer should keep a record which will enable her to identify which RCD is included for each and every deposit slip  to  give  assurance that all collection are deposited intact. And she should also reflect the reference no./date of the RCD on the reference portion of the cashbook for easy verification.
We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
Stop the issuance of receipts to “sub-collectors” for they are not bonded.
Maintain an adequate permanent record book which also contains the column “date reported totally used, sold, issued” to  readily determine the whereabouts of the Accountable Forms.
Require her collectors to prepare and file the Requisition and  	Issue Slip and avoid the borrowing or transfer of Accountable Forms 	between collectors or “sub collectors”.
Submit to the Office of the Auditor the Consolidated Report of Accountability of Accountable forms every 5th day of the ensuing month as required.
Report immediately loss of accountable forms and submit letter  	request for its relief as required under Sec 100, Book II, Vol I, GAAM.
Maintain separate cashbook for the DBP and LBP depository  	bank accounts under the General Fund of the Municipality and to reflect its beginning and ending balances every month.

We recommended that  Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly follow Section 43 and  Section 46 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs to facilitate verification of entries in the JEV, Check Disbursement Journal and Cash Disbursement Journal.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to comply strictly with the provisions of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 2009-006 to provide us the timely post-audit of the transactions.

We recommended that the Accounting and the General Services Office adopt systems and procedures required in the accounting and recording of PPEs for the fair presentation of this account in the financial statements.
The Accounting Department should:
Maintain PPELC for each category of  PPE as required in Section 114 of NGAS Manual. Start from the current year and backwards.
Monitor the submission of the Report of Physical count of PPE and periodically conduct reconciliation of accounts for immediate preparation of JEV on accounts that will require adjustments.
Provide depreciation for every PPE at year end
The General Services Office should:
As part of the inventory committee, prepare specific time table for the conduct of physical inventory of PPE to facilitate the timely submission of the RPCPPE to the Local Chief Executive and to COA.
Maintain  property cards for reconciliation with the accounting ledger cards.

We recommended that  Management abide by the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184 to avoid sanctions and penalties for non-compliance. In this regard, we recommend the following:
Submit the lacking documents enumerated in accordance with the GPPB prescribed forms under the Philippine Bidding Documents 4th Edition, the Revised IRR of RA 9184, and COA Circular 2009-001 in order to facilitate technical review and evaluation.
Strictly observe and execute the duties of the BAC and TWG as stipulated in the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184.

We recommended that Management strictly follow the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 regarding contracts and POs. We require the submission of the approved contracts/PO together with its complete supporting documents (bidding docs, performance security and other documents peculiar to the transaction such as those required by AO No. 233) of the abovementioned procurement to the Office of the Auditor immediately to enable us to review and make decisions in audit, otherwise transactions covered by the un-submitted documents may be suspended in audit.

We recommended that Management submit justification/explanation  	of the above mentioned transactions for evaluation and to immediately stop this practice.
We also recommended that the Municipality strictly follow the provisions under DECS-DBM-DILG JC No.1, as amended on the SEF utilization.

We recommended that Management observe strictly Section 100 of RA No. 7160 and the implementation of DECS-DBM-DILG JC no.1, as amended on the SEF utilization and to stop this practice immediately to avoid suspension/ disallowance.
We also recommended that Management submit a justification/explanation for those expenses which are contrary to the above mentioned Joint Circular.

We recommended that Management adequately plan the incoming projects of the Municipality to prevent undue delay upon implementation. We also recommended the timely submission of documents pertaining to time extensions of incoming projects to enable us to validate the reasonableness of the grounds for the issuance and the period of the requested extension of contract time for infrastructure project.
We have requested that Management provide COA with an update on the status of the titling of the lot where the New Municipal Hall building is currently being constructed.
We recommended that Management strictly follow the provisions of the IRR of RA No. 9184 and to prepare the above-mentioned documents for its procurement.

We recommended that Management support the transaction with complete documentation to validate the claim for financial assistance otherwise it will be suspended/disallowed in audit.
We also recommended that Management adopt systems and procedures in determining the amounts extended as financial assistance that would show fairness, equality and uniformity upon its constituents since resources in the municipality is limited.

We recommended that the granting of cash incentives awards to LGU Employees and Elective Officials should be in accordance with the existing Civil Service Commission policy on Program on Awards and Incentives for Service Excellence (PRAISE). We also recommended that Management strictly follow Article 77 and 170 (c) of the IRR of RA No. 7160 in payment of compensation and benefits to local officials and employees.
We further recommended that Management stop the payment of cash incentives to outgoing local officials and employees without legal basis to prevent disallowances.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the following necessary documents for evaluation to cause the settlement of the remaining unliquidated cash advances of the Municipality for CY 2015,to wit:

2 demand letters to officials and employees with remaining cash advances.
Certification from the Municipal Mayor or any other official that can certify that the said official or employee cannot be found nor traced.
And Death Certificate for deceased officials or employees.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary ledgers for each official and employee granted with cash advances to facilitate easy monitoring of balances to prevent the release of another cash advance without the settlement or proper accounting of the previous cash advance. The subsidiary ledgers should also be used as basis for the approval of clearances of officials or employees with cash advances.

Likewise, the standard form of the Report on Ageing of CA should be used by the Municipality illustrated in Annex 3 of Memorandum Circular No. 2012-02-A dated October 31, 2012, an Administrative Order No.25 S. 2011.
